extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR won from gruppo compatto on final Mur de Huy ascent after UAE Emirates controlled all day, holding off late-surging SKELMOSE by centimeters.
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates burned entire team chasing breakaway all day despite having strongest finisher, questionable energy expenditure pre-finale.
VERMARC bridged to breakaway final lap, stronger tactic than sitting in peloton given UAE's unsustainable chase tempo.
BARDET attacked outside on steepest section but WOODS unintentionally squeezed him, revealing positioning battles trump pure power.
Trek-Segafredo brought SKELMOSE and CICCONE both into top-5, multiple-card strategy nearly paid off against sitting Pogacar.

rider_signals.SKELMOSE Mattias: positive: Closed gap to Pogacar in final meters on seated climb, nearly won despite youth, genuine Ardennes contender emerging.
rider_signals.VERMARC Louis: positive: Only rider attempting bridge to breakaway final lap showed tactical creativity absent from passive peloton.
rider_signals.HEALY Ben: neutral: Positioned well with 1km to go but disappeared on steepest section, suggests lacking explosive punch.
rider_signals.BARDET Roman: negative: Attacked early on Mur but immediately neutralized by positioning squeeze, mistimed move before maximum gradient.
rider_signals.WOODS Mike: neutral: Led into steepest section for Israel-PT but faded to fourth, domestique effort disguised as leadership.
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: neutral: Strong leadout suggests climbing development but disappeared when racing started, still in development phase.
